1.5 Parametric Equations

Previously, we described lines in as intersections of planes. However, we may also describe a line as the trajectory of a moving point.

Suppose a line passes through the points

and

Let denote a point moving along the line such that

and

Then

Since

we obtain

Hence

Theorem 1.5.1.

The parametric equations of this line are

Remark.

In general, if a line passes through a point and has direction vector , then a parametric equation for the line is

Equivalently,

The constant terms determine a point on the line, while the coefficients of determine its direction.

Example 1.5.2 (Cycloid).

A circle of radius rolls along the -axis without slipping. Find a parametric equation for the path traced by a point on the rim of the circle. Describe the main geometric features of the resulting curve.

Solution. Let be the angle through which the circle has rotated. Since the circle rolls without slipping, the distance traveled by its center is equal to the arc length . Therefore the center of the circle is at

Relative to the center, the marked point begins at and rotates clockwise through angle , so its displacement from the center is

Hence the traced curve is

This curve is called a cycloid.

For , one obtains a single arch of the cycloid. The arch begins at , ends at , and reaches its highest point

The velocity vector is

so for the slope of the tangent line is

Thus the tangent is horizontal at the top point , while the endpoints are cusps.

The arc length of one arch is

The area under one arch is
